proposals.name: Skip-tolerant keystream decode (the page-73 mechanism generalized): DP/beam over key-advance skips, anchored backward from the solved AN-END page
proposals.rationale: Cicada's own solved LP2 page (73.jpg, AN END) used a phi(prime) keystream PLUS a skip mechanic (F-interrupters that consume no key). The dual mechanic — key elements consumed with no output, triggered when the output would repeat the previous rune — is precedented house style, reproduces BOTH diagnostic statistics (verified by simulation in this repo: phi(prime)+soft-skip gives IoC*N 1.000 and doublet 0.46-0.66% depending on suppression), and is invisible to every keystream/periodic-key test the project ran, because a raw-stream decrypt desyncs at the first skip (measured: median first-desync at position ~75, correct-rune rate 3.9% thereafter). Crucially the skip is ciphertext-checkable at decode time: a skip branch is only consistent when c[i-1]-c[i] = st[j]-st[j+1] mod 29 (~3.4% of positions), so the decode DP is nearly deterministic — branching factor ~1.03 — and a correct generator would light up unambiguously. The 83% softness even resembles a deterministic 5-in-6 rule or a by-hand discipline that slips 1-in-6.
proposals.fits_constraints: Verified by direct simulation using the repo's own lp.stats: uniform-mod-29 keystream (phi(primes)) + soft skip-on-collision yields IoC*N = 1.000 (flat) and doublet 0.46% at suppress=0.83 (0.66% at ~0.75) — the only proposed mechanism besides the project's resample-OTP model that hits both numbers, and unlike resample-OTP it is low-entropy and attackable. Short WORD keys fail the flat-IoC constraint (sim: IoC*N 1.204) so the sweep should be over uniform-coverage streams, not keywords.
proposals.how_to_test: 1) Build a DP decoder: state=(position i, stream index j); transitions j+1 (always) and j+2 (only when c[i-1]-c[i] = st[j]-st[j+1] mod 29, small log-penalty); emission = quadgram score of decoded rune. 2) Validate on synthetic: encrypt English with each generator + soft skip, confirm the DP recovers it and that wrong generators score in the noise band. 3) Sweep the Cicada-attested generator family (primes, phi(primes), iterated phi, prime gaps, Fibonacci seeds) x start offsets 0-1000 x both signs x +/-Atbash, per page AND as one continuous stream. 4) Sharpest sub-case: page 73's phi(prime) alignment is KNOWN — if the book is one continuous stream (the project's own STRUCTURE-FINDINGS evidence), run the DP backward from that anchored index across pages 55..0, absorbing unknown skip count (~430 expected) in the DP window. Also compose with the F-interrupter beam (plaintext nulls) the rig already has in solve.py.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: For any (generator, offset, sign, atbash): DP best-path score in the English band (score_norm -4.0 to -4.4, threshold -5.2) with readable Gematria-English, vs the noise band (~-6). Synthetic validation gives the exact expected separation. A full null over the attested generator family cleanly closes the skip variant and genuinely upgrades the OTP verdict.
proposals.already_covered_check: attack.py keystream tests raw streams only (read the code: pure additive, no skip branching); vigauto is a fixed-period column attack that my simulation shows is blinded by phase drift; the interrupter beam in attack_vigenere handles plaintext F-nulls only, not key-side skips; OPEN-AVENUES section 2 explicitly dismissed collision-skip with the claim it is 'approximately plain Vigenere except at doublet positions' — that claim is wrong because each skip permanently shifts key phase for all subsequent positions.

proposals.name: Doublet forensics: the 86 tolerated doublets as a fingerprint or side-channel
proposals.rationale: Campaign XI quantified the doublet RATE (0.664% = ~83% suppression) but nobody has analyzed the doublets themselves. I extracted them: exactly 86 doublets at positions [122, 207, 456, 653, ...], per-page counts 0-5, all rune values doubled except value 17. If the filter is honest rejection sampling their positions are uniform; if the softness is deterministic (e.g. every-6th-key-element exemption, or positions chosen to MARK something) the positions carry structure — and 86 marked positions is exactly the size of a short hidden message or a parameter table.
proposals.fits_constraints: This analyzes the observed statistics rather than assuming a mechanism, so it is consistent with flat IoC and the deficit by construction. Any positive finding would refine WHICH filter model holds; a null still discriminates rejection-sampling from deterministic exemption (constrains hand vs tool generation).
proposals.how_to_test: All cheap, one script: (a) KS test of the 86 positions against uniform, and gap distribution against geometric; (b) chi-square of the 86 doubled VALUES against uniform; (c) cross-reference positions with word boundaries, line starts, F-rune adjacency, and page offsets; (d) gaps and positions mod 29 / mod small n as rune indices, scored for English; (e) the 86 doubled runes read in order as an 86-rune string through identity/Atbash/gematria; (f) positions vs primes and vs the pp49-51 payload interpreted as a gap table (see proposal 4).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Distribution tests: p < 0.001 non-uniformity = structure exists. Decode tests: score_norm in the English band. Otherwise: a measured 'positions are uniform, values are uniform' null that pins the filter as memoryless rejection — itself a publishable refinement of Campaign XI.
proposals.already_covered_check: Campaign XI measured only the aggregate rate; STRUCTURE-FINDINGS analyzed F-rune positions (a different object) and separator-type doublet rates but never the doublet positions/values themselves; no ledger row touches them.

proposals.name: Preserved plaintext metadata: word-length / punctuation / layout channel (and the English-shape test the whole project silently depends on)
proposals.rationale: Under any additive keystream, rune identities are randomized but the word separators, punctuation, line breaks, and page layout are AUTHORED PLAINTEXT-SIDE metadata that survives encryption untouched. Two distinct payoffs: (1) diagnostic — if unsolved-page word-length distribution matches solved-page English-in-runes, the plaintext really is English prose (validating every English-scored sweep) and yields crib geometry (single-rune words, long words) as priors for proposal 1's DP; if it does NOT match, the plaintext is not English-shaped and 100+ eliminations were scored against the wrong language model — a project-level correction. (2) channel — Cicada is exactly the kind of author to hide content in the metadata stream itself (word lengths as digits, punctuation as delimiters, line-initial runes as an acrostic).
proposals.fits_constraints: Fully consistent: it makes no claim about the rune stream at all — flat IoC and the doublet deficit are untouched whether the metadata carries content or not. An acrostic forced in CIPHERTEXT would locally dent uniformity, which is itself the test in (c).
proposals.how_to_test: (a) chi-square word-length distribution of the 55 pages vs solved-page plaintext word lengths vs random-segmentation null; (b) word-length sequence and punctuation sequence as base-10/base-29 digit streams -> primes/ASCII/runes, scored; (c) uniformity test on line-initial, word-initial, and page-initial ciphertext runes vs the global uniform distribution (any forcing = non-uniform); (d) per-page word counts and line counts as an integer sequence vs primes/OEIS; (e) word-length n-gram matching against an LP1 phrase bank (A WARNING, AN END, THE PRIMES ARE SACRED...) to generate positional crib hypotheses feeding proposal 1.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: (a) is decisive either way: match (p > 0.05 vs solved pages) = English plaintext confirmed; mismatch (p < 0.001) = the scoring assumption is broken. (b)/(d): any decode over threshold. (c): p < 0.001 non-uniformity at line/page starts.
proposals.already_covered_check: STRUCTURE-FINDINGS covers F-counts, separator-type doublet rates, and per-page IoC — not word-length distributions, not punctuation-stream decodes, not initial-rune uniformity; run_stats.py computes entropy/IoC only; no ledger row tests whether the plaintext is even English-shaped.

proposals.name: pp49-51 payload as per-page/meta PARAMETERS: page permutation, keystream offsets, or the doublet-gap table
proposals.rationale: The payload was attacked as key, text, hash, container, image — always as CONTENT. Never as METADATA about the other 53 pages. 256 bytes is the right size for a parameter table: 56 page-order values, 53-56 per-page keystream start offsets, or a delta-encoded position list — and I now have exactly 86 doublet positions (85 gaps, max 712, fitting 16-bit values: 128 available) to compare against directly.
proposals.fits_constraints: Makes no mechanism claim about the rune stream; per-page offsets compose with proposal 1 (offsets into a shared generator preserve flat IoC and the skip-produced deficit). Page-permutation readings only claim the reading ORDER differs, which the underpowered 54-pair boundary test (see flawed assumptions) does not exclude.
proposals.how_to_test: (a) instant: slide a 56-byte window over the payload, test each for being a permutation of 0-55 (mod-56 and rank-order variants); (b) instant: read the payload as 85-128 gap values (8-bit, 16-bit LE/BE, varint) and correlate against the real doublet-position gaps [122, 85, 249, 197, 129, ...]; (c) cheap: use bytes 0-55 (and windows) as per-page start offsets into each generator of proposal 1's DP sweep; (d) bytes mod 29 as per-page Atbash/shift toggles feeding the same DP.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: (a) a valid permutation window (probability ~10^-24 by chance per window — unambiguous); (b) rank correlation with observed doublet gaps, |r| > 0.5 with p < 10^-6; (c)/(d) English-band DP scores. All tests have hard numeric gates; expected outcome is null and that is fine — each is minutes of compute.
proposals.already_covered_check: Campaign VII tested the payload as a polyalphabetic KEY over the runes (337,944 configs) and as text/prime/RSA; IX/XII tested hash structure, format, XOR, image. No campaign tested it as a page permutation, as per-page offsets into an external generator, or against the doublet-position list (which was never extracted until now).

proposals.name: Reader-supplied procedural keys: Cicada-attested TRANSFORMS of the solved-page plaintext as keystreams
proposals.rationale: The ledger tested solved plaintext as a direct running key. But Cicada's demonstrated key style is never raw text — it is text passed through the gematria machinery (DIVINITY as shift values; CIRCUMFERENCE re-spelled through F; primes and phi as first-class operations; 'the primes are sacred'). The intended key may be a short PROCEDURE applied to material every solver already has: e.g. keystream = phi(gematria-prime of each solved-plaintext rune) mod 29, or cumulative gematria sums mod 29, or the prime value stream of LP1's plaintext. That is a small, enumerable, Cicada-canonical transform set — not an open-ended text hunt.
proposals.fits_constraints: These streams have near-uniform mod-29 coverage (prime values mod 29 are equidistributed), so flat IoC holds; the doublet deficit comes from the same skip/filter layer as proposal 1 (compose each derived stream with the skip-DP). A raw derived stream alone would fail the deficit — which is why it must be run through the skip decoder, not the plain runningkey attack.
proposals.how_to_test: Enumerate the transform set: {identity, Atbash} x {gematria prime value, phi(prime), index, cumulative sum} x {LP1 plaintext, solved LP2 plaintext, the rune poem, Gematria Primus table order} — a few dozen streams. Run each through the validated runningkey harness AND through proposal 1's skip-DP, all offsets, both signs. Strictly cap the set at Cicada-attested operations to avoid open-ended dredging.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: score_norm over -5.2 with readable English on any page vs the -6 noise band; synthetic validation of the harness on a known transform first. A null closes the 'procedural key from solver-held material' class with named, enumerated members.
proposals.already_covered_check: The ledger's row is 'solved-page plaintext as running key' — the raw text only. Number-theoretic streams tested were plaintext-INDEPENDENT (primes, phi, Fibonacci). Streams DERIVED from solver-held text via gematria/prime transforms appear nowhere in the ledger, and none were composed with skip semantics.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The no-repeat filter destroys information (resample-from-fresh-pad semantics), making the pages OTP-class and information-theoretically unsolvable.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: A skip-forward filter (advance a DETERMINISTIC keystream past elements that would produce a doublet) yields the identical observable signature — I verified in-repo that phi(prime)+soft-skip gives IoC*N 1.000 and doublet 0.46-0.66% — while remaining a low-entropy, decryptable cipher. Cicada's own page-73 solve already uses key-advance-skip mechanics (F-interrupters). The 'OTP + filter' language quietly commits to the one filter implementation that is unbreakable without evidence distinguishing it from the one that is breakable.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: The headline verdict ('information-theoretically unsolvable without an external key') is premature: an entire attackable mechanism class, precedented by the puzzle's own solved pages, has never been tested.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: OPEN-AVENUES section 2: 'collision-skip is approximately plain Vigenere except at the ~0.66% doublet positions, so auto-Vigenere already covered it.'
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Each skip shifts the key phase for ALL subsequent positions, not just locally; skips occur at ~3% of positions so phase drifts hundreds of steps over the corpus. My simulation confirms: with skips, column IoC at the true key length collapses from 1.78 to ~1.2, and a raw unfiltered decrypt is 3.9% correct after a median first-desync at position ~75 — vigauto and attack_keystream are structurally blind to it.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: The eliminations of periodic keys (len 1-40) and all number-theoretic/PRNG keystreams only hold for no-skip alignment; the family is open, not closed.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: English quadgram scoring (threshold -5.2) is a valid null detector for every sweep.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Every elimination is really a (hypothesis x English-plaintext) elimination. LP2's plaintext could be Old English (the runes' native language), Latin, or gematria-encoded numbers/primes — all of which score in the noise band under an English model. The solved pages being English is suggestive, not probative, for the deliberately harder second book.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Silent false nulls across vigauto, keystream, autokey, and payload-as-key sweeps. Cheap mitigation: re-score the already-computed best decrypts under OE/Latin n-gram models, and run proposal 3(a) to test whether the word-length profile is even English-shaped.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The keystream is one continuous stream over the whole book with no reset (STRUCTURE-FINDINGS section 2, from 0 doublets across 54 page joins).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: The test is underpowered by construction: expected cross-join doublets are ~0.36 if the rule is active and ~1.9 if inactive; observing 0 has probability ~15% even under the inactive hypothesis. Stated as established fact, it is a ~1.5-sigma hint.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Per-page independent keys, per-page offsets (proposal 4c), and page-order permutations are not actually excluded; 'no boundary where the keystream restarts' should not steer future attacks as a constraint.

overclaimed_as_closed: 'Every periodic key, length 1-40' — vigauto is a fixed-period column-frequency attack with no interrupter/skip handling; it cannot see phase-drifting keys (simulation: column IoC flattens to ~1.2 under skips). Closed only for no-skip Vigenere.

'Number-theoretic keystreams (primes, phi, ...) — nothing' — tested raw/unfiltered only; a skip-filtered variant of the SAME generators desyncs such tests at median position ~75 and reproduces both LP2 statistics exactly. The family the solved AN-END page actually used (keystream + skips) was never run against LP2 with skip branching.

OPEN-AVENUES: 'Collision-skip / constrained Viterbi ... the all-key-length auto-Vigenere already covered it' — mathematically incorrect (skips shift phase cumulatively); this one sentence is where the attackable variant was waved off.
'The keystream is CONTINUOUS across the whole book (no boundary reset)' — presented as a finding; the 54-pair evidence cannot distinguish active vs inactive at page joins (P(0 | inactive) ~ 15%).

'Crib-dragging — dead' — crib-induced key fragments were presumably tested under no-skip alignment; under skip semantics every fragment past the first skip is misaligned, so the null does not transfer to the skip class.

The one-paragraph verdict 'information-theoretically unsolvable without the external key' — conditional on resample-filter semantics that were assumed, not demonstrated; overclaims until the skip-tolerant decode of the attested generator family is run and nulled.
highest_ev_experiment: Build and run the skip-tolerant keystream decoder (proposal 1). Concretely: DP over states (position i, stream index j) with transitions j+1 (no-skip) and j+2 (skip, allowed only when c[i-1]-c[i] = st[j]-st[j+1] mod 29 — ciphertext-checkable, ~3.4% of positions, so the search is nearly deterministic); emission = the rig's own quadgram score; validate on synthetic skip-encrypted English first; then sweep primes/phi(primes)/iterated-phi/Fibonacci x offsets 0-1000 x sign x Atbash, per page and as one continuous stream anchored BACKWARD from page 73's known phi(prime) alignment; optionally compose with the existing F-interrupter beam. This is the highest-EV move because (a) it is the only proposal whose mechanism is already verified to reproduce BOTH diagnostic statistics (IoC*N 1.000, doublet ~0.5-0.7% — I simulated it with the repo's own lp.stats), (b) it is precedented by Cicada's actual solved-page mechanics, (c) it attacks a demonstrated hole in the elimination ledger rather than extending a closed family, and (d) either outcome is decisive: a hit solves pages; a null converts the project's headline verdict from 'assumed resample semantics' to 'skip semantics tested and excluded', making the OTP claim genuinely rigorous.
verdict_on_unsolvable: Overstated — but narrowly and fixably. The project's statistics are sound, the transcription work is solid, and the eliminations of autokey, unfiltered running keys, fractionation, substitution, and transposition are genuinely conclusive. However, the load-bearing step from 'flat IoC + doublet deficit' to 'one-time-pad-class, information-theoretically unsolvable' silently assumes the no-repeat filter is resample-based (fresh pad entropy consumed at each rejection). The skip-based alternative — advance a deterministic keystream past collision-producing elements, exactly the key-consumption mechanic Cicada already used with F-interrupters on the solved AN-END page — reproduces both diagnostic statistics precisely (verified by simulation in the repo: IoC*N 1.000, doublet 0.46-0.66%), is decryptable by a recipient, is invisible to every test in the ledger (raw-stream decrypts desync at median position ~75), and was dismissed in OPEN-AVENUES with an argument that is mathematically wrong about phase behavior. Until the skip-tolerant decode of the Cicada-attested generator family is run and nulled, the honest verdict is 'OTP-class OR skip-filtered deterministic keystream — the former unsolvable, the latter attackable and untested.' If that sweep nulls (a real possibility; prior is medium at best), the unsolvable verdict stands as the best-supported conclusion for every mechanism anyone has been able to construct.

lens: Highest-expected-value untried experiment + explicit falsification criteria for the 'externally-keyed OTP / built-to-be-unsolvable' framing + audit of over-claimed closures

proposals.name: Plaintext-side word-boundary forensics (the channel an OTP cannot protect)
proposals.rationale: The corpus preserves word separators '-' (~2,645 words), clause marks '.', and line breaks '/'. Under ANY additive cipher — including a true OTP with an output filter — these are UNENCRYPTED plaintext metadata: the sequence of word lengths survives encryption untouched. Three arms: (1) compare the unsolved pages' word-length distribution (KS test, Zipf profile, per-page) against rune-encoded English derived from the SOLVED LP plaintexts — this directly tests whether an English message exists under the pad at all; (2) slide-match each page's word-length SEQUENCE against the 112 already-fetched keytexts re-cast as candidate PLAINTEXTS (encoded via the rig's digraph tokenizer, and letterwise), with significance from a decoy-text null distribution — if Cicada enciphered a known public passage under a random pad, running-key attacks are blind to it but word-length fingerprinting finds it, and a hit yields the full pad by subtraction (total break); (3) test whether separators are even plausibly real (e.g., 1-rune and 2-rune word frequencies vs English function-word statistics).
proposals.fits_constraints: Fully consistent by construction: word-length metadata is orthogonal to symbol statistics. A filtered-OTP over English with real separators produces exactly IoC·N 1.000 and 0.66% doublets AND English-like word lengths; a hand-generated decoy produces the same symbol stats but arbitrary word lengths. The test does not propose an alternative cipher — it interrogates the one channel the established mechanism leaves open.
proposals.how_to_test: Extract word-length sequences from data/krisyotam_runes.txt (split on -/./%); build the English-in-runes reference from solved-page plaintexts and from corpus texts encoded with src/lp/gematria.keyword_to_indices; run KS/chi-square on distributions; run sliding exact+fuzzy sequence matching (edit distance on length vectors) of each page against each candidate text with permutation-based p-values. Pure Python over existing repo data; an afternoon of work.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Arm 1: KS statistic inside vs outside the solved-page/English band (either outcome is decisive: match = a real English plaintext exists, framing survives but 'no purchase' weakens; mismatch = plaintext is not ordinary segmented English, so every English-scored elimination is conditional and the decoy hypothesis gains). Arm 2: any page whose word-length sequence matches a public text above the decoy null (p < 1e-3) — verify by subtracting to recover the pad and checking the pad's own statistics.
proposals.already_covered_check: The ledger's only boundary work is doublet-rate-by-separator (STRUCTURE-FINDINGS §2, keystream continuity) and 'word-boundary positional bias' of doublet positions (Campaign IV) — both key-side. No file compares word-length distributions to English or matches length sequences against texts (grep for word-length analysis returns only an image-alignment comment in structure/pipeline.py).

proposals.name: Total-structure closure: cross-validated compression / sequence-model test vs matched filtered-uniform controls
proposals.rationale: The 'OTP-class, no internal purchase' verdict rests entirely on order-<=3 statistics: monogram IoC, bigram chi-square, trigram IoC, autocorrelation lags 2-30, order-0 entropy. 'No computable structure' is asserted far beyond what was measured. A stateful PRNG, a human-generated pad (humans are notoriously compressible RNGs — the lag-1 repeat-avoidance already looks like the classic human bias), or any higher-order generation artifact would pass every current test yet leave the stream compressible. Train order-k context models / LZMA / a small neural next-rune predictor on LP2 with cross-validation and compare bits-per-rune against an ensemble of synthetic controls generated by the project's own soft-83%-filter model (which match IoC 1.000 and 0.66% doublets exactly).
proposals.fits_constraints: The controls are constructed to reproduce both signature statistics precisely, so the test measures only structure BEYOND the two constraints — it cannot contradict them, it audits what they leave unexamined.
proposals.how_to_test: Generate 1,000 filtered-uniform 12,956-rune controls with campaign11's suppression model (seeded); compute cross-validated bits/rune for LP2 vs the control band using (a) KT/PPM context models orders 1-8, (b) LZMA on packed indices, (c) optionally a tiny transformer. Also add the two provenance sub-tests Campaign IV skipped: sliding-window dispersion (multinomial vs under-dispersed — machine sampler vs human/deck generation) and monogram frequency drift across the book (fatigue signature).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: LP2 bits/rune below the 1st percentile of the control band (or predictor accuracy significantly above 1/29-adjusted baseline) = computable structure exists = the OTP verdict is WRONG and there is an attack surface. LP2 inside the band = the strongest possible ciphertext-side confirmation ever run — upgrades 'OTP-class' from low-order-verified to model-class-verified.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger has Shannon entropy (order-0 only), trigram IoC, bigram chi-square, lags 2-30, geometric doublet gaps, Poisson per-page counts. No compression test, no context-model/ML predictor, no windowed-dispersion or drift test appears anywhere in analysis/.

proposals.name: Full-lag self-coincidence scan (lags 31-6,478) + corpus-wide long-period attack (periods 41-2,000)
proposals.rationale: The periodicity closure has a real hole. Campaign IV's autocorrelation stops at lag 30; attack.py vigauto runs PER PAGE with maxlen=40 — on ~230-rune pages a length-40 key leaves ~6 samples per column, so the column chi-square attack is underpowered from roughly L=20 up, and the project's OWN finding that the keystream is continuous across the entire book (STRUCTURE-FINDINGS §2) means the correct search unit is the 12,956-rune concatenation, which was never scanned. A book-wide repeated key of length 41-2,000 would give ~6.1% shifted-coincidence at its period (vs 3.45% baseline) with thousands of pairs — trivially detectable — and IoC·N of 1+0.78/p (<=1.008 for p>=100) is inside the measurement noise of the reported 'perfect 1.000'.
proposals.fits_constraints: IoC: periods >=~80 are observationally indistinguishable from 1.000 at n=12,956. Doublets: the 0.66% deficit is produced by the output-side no-repeat filter that EVERY surviving model already requires (Campaigns X/XI showed the filter acts on the written output, not the key) — the filter is orthogonal to key periodicity, so 'periodic key + the same output filter' reproduces both statistics.
proposals.how_to_test: One script: coincidence rate c(L) = P(c_i == c_{i+L}) over the full concatenation for every L in 1-6,478 with binomial error bars; flag any L above 4%. If a spike appears, run the existing column-frequency attack corpus-wide at that period (both signs, +Atbash), with scoring made tolerant to ~2.7% filter-perturbed positions.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any lag with coincidence rate significantly above 3.45% (>4 sigma) = key reuse/periodicity = the OTP verdict is falsified and the page is breakable. All-flat = the 'every periodic key' claim finally becomes true for all periods, corpus-wide, instead of 1-40 per-page.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger row says 'every periodic key, length 1-40' — code shows this is per-page (attack.py:202-224) and underpowered at the top of that range; autocorrelation was measured only at lags 2-30 (Campaign IV §3); inter-page in-depth was tested only at page-aligned offsets, not arbitrary lags.

proposals.name: Second-tokenization running-key re-sweep (letterwise + prime-value mappings)
proposals.rationale: Every one of the 112+ keytext eliminations encoded the key with gematria.keyword_to_indices, which is greedy digraph-first (TH->one rune, plus fixed aliases Q->C, K->C, V->U, Z->S). If Cicada keyed letterwise (T,H as two runes — equally plausible; both conventions appear in community work), every tested text was tested at the WRONG rune sequence and all 112 nulls are conditional on one tokenization. Note the mechanistic 'running keys are dead because English keys inject 3.3% doublets' argument does NOT rescue this: under the project's own output-filter model only ~2.7% of positions are perturbed, so decrypting with the TRUE text-key would still yield ~97% English and score far above threshold — meaning the sweep WOULD have caught the right text, but only in the tokenization actually tried. Also untested: keying by gematria prime values mod 29 instead of indices (Campaign V excluded multiplicative SUBSTITUTION, not prime-valued running keys).
proposals.fits_constraints: Identical consistency story to the ledger's own running-key attacks: the doublet deficit and flat IoC come from the ct-side no-repeat filter layered on the running key; a filtered running-key ciphertext still decrypts to ~97% English under the true key, so the attack remains valid and both statistics remain explained.
proposals.how_to_test: Add a letterwise_to_indices variant (and a prime-value-mod-29 variant) beside keyword_to_indices; re-run attack.py runningkey over the already-fetched 112-text corpus (data/keys/, campaign12/13 fetchers) — pure re-execution, no new data. Threshold and scoring unchanged.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any page/text/offset scoring above the validated -5.2 threshold (English -4.0 to -4.4); the existing selftest already proves the pipeline recovers planted keys.
proposals.already_covered_check: gematria.py:95-113 shows only the greedy-digraph parser exists; no letterwise or prime-value key mapping appears in attack.py, src/lp/, or any campaign script. The 112 eliminations are therefore '112 texts x 1 encoding', not '112 texts'.

proposals.name: pp49-51 payload as RSA signature/ciphertext under known Cicada public keys
proposals.rationale: The payload is EXACTLY 2048 bits, high-entropy, and even — which rules out a modulus (as Campaign VII noted) but is precisely the shape of an RSA-2048 SIGNATURE or ciphertext. Cicada published RSA public keys (PGP key 7A35090F and puzzle-embedded moduli from 2012-2013). Signature verification needs only the PUBLIC key: compute s^e mod n for every known Cicada modulus (both byte orders, e=65537 and e=3) and check for PKCS#1 v1.5 / PSS / OAEP padding structure or a recognizable digest. A hit would prove the payload is authenticated 3301 material and reveal WHAT it signs (possibly the AN END hash or a key fingerprint). If it is instead an RSA ciphertext to a solver-held key, that is unrecoverable but would sharpen the 'externally-keyed by design' story.
proposals.fits_constraints: The payload is a separate non-runic object; the runic statistics (IoC 1.000, 0.66% doublets) place no constraint on it. No conflict possible.
proposals.how_to_test: Collect all RSA moduli from Cicada's published PGP keys (keyservers/archives already mirrored in analysis/osint/) and the 2012/2013 puzzle RSA values; for each (n, e) with n > payload-as-int, compute pow(s, e, n) in both endiannesses and pattern-match PKCS#1/PSS/known-digest structure. Minutes of compute.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: pow(s, e, n) yielding 0x0001FF..FF00||DigestInfo (or valid PSS trailer 0xBC) for any known Cicada modulus — unambiguous, zero-false-positive structure. Clean nulls close the last natural 2048-bit interpretation of the blob.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger/Campaigns VII, IX, XII tested: primality/modulus-shape, key-over-runes, text, container formats, hash preimages, repeating XOR, image rasters. Signature VERIFICATION under known public keys (payload as s, not as n) appears nowhere.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The plaintext is English prose (all attack scoring uses English monogram/quadgram models with threshold -5.2; every 'null' is a failure to produce ENGLISH).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Solved pages are English, but the unsolved book could be Latin, Old English, Enochian-style constructed text, or non-prose structured content (spelled-out numbers, coordinates). Nothing internal verifies the language; the preserved word-length metadata that could test it has never been analyzed.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: All 112+ running-key eliminations, the periodic-key nulls, and the per-page polymorphic battery are conditional nulls — the right key could already have been tried and discarded because its decrypt scored as 'gibberish' under an English model.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The doublet deficit was produced by filtering the KEYSTREAM's output during encryption (the ledger's stated mechanism).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Three generative models are observationally equivalent from the ciphertext: (a) filter-at-encryption, (b) post-hoc editing of ~2.7% of ciphertext positions after encryption with ANY plaintext-independent key, (c) no plaintext at all — a hand- or machine-generated repeat-avoiding stream (decoy or a printed pad for something else). Repeat-avoidance is also the classic human random-generation bias, not necessarily 'engineering'.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Under (b), the mechanistic exclusion of plaintext-independent keys ('they would inject 3.3% doublets') collapses — long periodic keys and untested PRNGs become live again and are excluded only by the bounded searches actually run (1-40 per page, lags 2-30). Under (c), there is no message in these pages and the entire solve framing is aimed at the wrong object.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: 'Every periodic key, length 1-40' is eliminated, and flatness at lags 2-30 excludes periodicity.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: attack.py vigauto runs per page (~230 runes), so length-30-40 keys have 6-8 samples per column — the chi-square column attack has near-zero power there; and the project's own STRUCTURE finding says the stream is continuous across the whole book, making the 12,956-rune concatenation the correct search unit, which was never scanned for periods >30.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: A book-wide key of length ~41-2,000 (IoC·N <= 1.008-1.02, inside measurement noise of the reported 1.000) combined with the ct-side filter reproduces both signature statistics and would have been completely missed.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: Keytext-to-rune conversion is unique (greedy digraph parsing, Q->C/K->C/V->U/Z->S aliases).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Letterwise encoding (T+H as two runes) is an equally plausible authorial convention; gematria prime-values mod 29 is another plausible key mapping. Only greedy-digraph index arithmetic was ever run.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Each of the 112 'eliminated' keytexts was eliminated in only one of several plausible encodings; the true keytext could be in the already-tested corpus.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: 'Statistically a one-time pad' / 'no internal purchase' — i.e., no computable structure exists in the stream.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: The claim is supported only by order-<=3 statistics (IoC, bigram chi-square, trigram IoC, lags 2-30, order-0 entropy). No compression test, context-model, or ML predictor has ever been run; stateful PRNGs and human-generated pads pass all low-order tests while remaining compressible/predictable.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: The information-theoretic 'unsolvable' verdict would be false: exploitable higher-order structure could exist and has simply never been looked for with adequate tools.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: Transcription is 'verified correct 3 independent ways'.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Campaign IV itself concedes the three lineages share a common origin (consensus, not correctness), the image-independent template matcher failed its own gate (69.5% < 90%), AI vision failed (alignment 0.145), and page 24:172 remains unresolved. Verification is really 'single-lineage + spot audits + a 99.2% classifier corroboration'.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: A low-rate systematic misread (e.g., AE/A, double-stem runes) shared by all lineages would slightly depress every attack score; mostly a minor risk since solved pages from the same lineage decode cleanly, but the ledger's 'verified 3 ways' phrasing overstates independence.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: 'Substitution/homophonic dead because substitution preserves IoC.'
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: That reasoning is only valid for simple substitution — homophonic substitution is DESIGNED to flatten IoC, and cyclic homophone selection even suppresses doublets. The conclusion happens to survive anyway, but only because Campaign IV's off-diagonal bigram flatness (chi2=810 vs df=812) excludes it — English bigram structure would leak into the 29x29 table under any small-alphabet homophonic scheme.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: No practical consequence (the right measurement exists elsewhere in the repo), but the ledger row cites the wrong reason, which matters for anyone auditing the logic chain.

overclaimed_as_closed: 'Every periodic key, length 1-40' — actually per-page only (underpowered above ~L=20 on ~230-rune pages), never corpus-wide despite the project's own finding that the keystream is continuous across the whole book; autocorrelation checked only at lags 2-30, so periods 31+ were never scanned at all.

'~112 running-key texts eliminated' — eliminated under exactly one tokenization (greedy digraph via keyword_to_indices) and one key mapping (index arithmetic); letterwise encoding and prime-value-mod-29 mapping variants of the same texts are untested, and all nulls are additionally conditional on English-plaintext scoring.

'Transcription verified 3 ways' — the three lineages are byte-identical because they share one origin; both image-independent verification attempts (template matcher, AI vision) failed their own quality gates, so this is consensus-plus-spot-audit, not triple independent verification (Campaign IV says this honestly; the ledger row compresses it into an over-claim).

'The mechanism IS a filtered keystream' — filter-at-encryption, post-hoc ciphertext editing, and no-plaintext hand-generation (decoy/pad) are observationally equivalent from the ciphertext and only the first is asserted; the mechanistic exclusion of plaintext-independent keys silently depends on which of these is true.

'OTP-class / no internal structure' — established only up to order-3 statistics and lag 30; no compression, context-model, or ML-predictor test was ever run, so 'information-theoretically unsolvable' extrapolates beyond the measurements.

'Substitution/homophonic ruled out because substitution preserves IoC' — wrong reason for homophonic (which flattens IoC by design); the exclusion actually rests on Campaign IV's off-diagonal bigram flatness.
highest_ev_experiment: Plaintext-side word-boundary forensics. Defense: a one-time pad protects symbol VALUES but not metadata, and the unsolved corpus preserves word separators, clause marks, and line breaks that no analysis in the entire ledger has ever treated as data. One afternoon of stdlib code yields three decisive readouts: (1) whether the word-length distribution matches rune-encoded English from the solved pages — this is the only available test of the load-bearing, never-verified assumption that an English message exists under the pad at all (if it matches, the 'real message, externally keyed' framing gains its first plaintext-side evidence; if it doesn't, every English-scored elimination in the ledger becomes conditional and the hand-generated-decoy hypothesis gains sharply — either branch materially rewrites the project's conclusions); (2) sliding word-length-sequence matching against the 112 already-fetched texts recast as candidate PLAINTEXTS — the one attack class that works AGAINST a true OTP, with a small but real total-break tail (a match identifies the plaintext, the pad falls out by subtraction, and the recovered pad itself becomes analyzable); (3) a realism check on the separators themselves (1- and 2-rune word statistics vs English function words). No other untried experiment has this property that BOTH outcomes are high-information while also carrying a nonzero break probability at near-zero cost. Runner-up (and the cleanest pure falsifier): the cross-validated compression/sequence-model test against filter-matched controls, which converts 'OTP-class' from an order-3 extrapolation into a measured claim.
verdict_on_unsolvable: Agree with the ciphertext-symbol-level verdict, but the totalizing framing is mildly OVERSTATED in three specific ways. (1) The 'information-theoretically unsolvable' claim is proven only against the statistics actually measured (order <=3, lags <=30, periods <=40 per page); the definitive tests — total-structure compression/prediction against filter-matched controls, corpus-wide full-lag scan — have not been run, so 'no computable structure' is currently an extrapolation, albeit a plausible one. (2) 'Unsolvable without the external key' quietly assumes the plaintext is original prose: an OTP does not hide word-length metadata, and if the plaintext were a recognizable public text the pages would be solvable from public information with no key at all — an avenue the project has never touched, so the unsolvability claim is broader than the evidence. (3) The framing conflates three observationally equivalent worlds — externally-keyed real message, post-edited ciphertext, and keyless hand-generated decoy ('built to be unsolvable' in a much darker sense: nothing to solve) — and presents the first as the conclusion. What would falsify the framing: LP2 compressing or predicting better than IoC/doublet-matched synthetic controls (structure exists, not OTP); any coincidence spike at any lag over the full concatenation (key reuse, breakable); a word-length sequence match to a public text (solvable without the key); or a decode above the English threshold from the tokenization/language variants the current nulls are conditional on. Conversely, if the compression test, full-lag scan, and word-length forensics all come back null-and-English-like, the project would for the first time be entitled to the full strength of its own claim.

lens: assumptions — is the "OTP-class / information-theoretically unsolvable" verdict sound, and which unstated load-bearing assumptions, if false, reopen the problem. (Note: I did not just theorize — I ran six cheap probes against the repo data during this review; new empirical results are cited inline: suppression is uniform across word/line boundaries (0.62% internal vs 0.80% cross-'•' vs 0.74% cross-line, all ~5x suppressed); exact-match-only (delta±1 adjacency 6.87% ≈ random 6.90%); no lag-2/3 alternation excess (3.40%/3.39%); doublet rune-composition perfectly uniform (chi2 26.0, df 28); no positional periodicity mod 2..7; no first/second-half drift (0.615%/0.719%); 0/54 page-boundary doublets (expect 1.9 unsuppressed) — suppression appears to continue ACROSS page breaks, implying the body was generated as one continuous stream in book order; doublet-gap-as-message channel tested and NULL (-7.43 vs control -7.51±0.26); repeated-ciphertext-word count exactly at chance (481 vs 482.9±8.9) killing any fixed codebook/nomenclator; and word-length distribution of unsolved pages statistically MATCHES solved English pages (chi2 6.3, df~11) — an unexploited clue.)

proposals.name: Word-length skeleton attack (the metadata channel the rig never enumerates)
proposals.rationale: Every attack in the ledger treats the flat rune stream; runes_to_indices() strips the '•' separators and no analysis file mentions word lengths. But solved LP pages encipher word-by-word — ciphertext word boundaries ARE plaintext word boundaries. I measured the unsolved corpus: 3,316 words, mean 3.91 runes, and the length distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the solved English pages (chi2 6.3, df~11). If boundaries are plaintext-true (as on every solved page), the FULL plaintext word-length sequence of all 54 pages is already public, key-independent, and untouched. A ~20-word length window carries ~40-50 bits — enough to uniquely fingerprint a passage in any published text, no key needed.
proposals.fits_constraints: Uses only metadata (separator positions), so it is orthogonal to — and fully consistent with — flat IoC 1.000 and the 0.66% doublet deficit; a filtered pad over word-preserving encipherment produces exactly the observed rune statistics while leaking the word skeleton.
proposals.how_to_test: (1) Convert candidate corpora (the 112 already-fetched keytexts are sitting in analysis/campaign12-13 — reuse them as PLAINTEXT candidates, which nobody did) to rune-word lengths via the Gematria digraph compression (TH/EO/NG/OE/AE/IA/EA); (2) slide each unsolved page's length sequence over each corpus with exact and ±1-tolerance matching; (3) calibrate false-positive rate on shuffled skeletons. Separately: compare the skeleton's bigram/positional statistics (1-rune-word placement, sentence-like rhythm) against solved-page plaintexts vs a decoy-generator null.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: A corpus passage whose rune-word-length sequence matches a page beyond the shuffled-control FPR → instant plaintext candidate → subtract to get the key and inspect it. Clean null over large corpora is also informative: plaintext is original composition (like the koans), or the boundaries are decoys — either way the skeleton's English-likeness must then be explained.
proposals.already_covered_check: ELIMINATION-LEDGER has no row for word boundaries/lengths; attack.py and src/lp/stats.py operate on separator-stripped index streams; Kasiski trigrams are the only repeated-structure test ever run. All 112 keytext runs used texts as KEY, never as skeleton-matched PLAINTEXT.

proposals.name: Homophonic-downward / variable-rate encoding search (the ledger's substitution row eliminates the wrong class)
proposals.rationale: The ledger kills substitution/homophonic with 'substitution preserves IoC; can't turn flat 1.00 into English 1.78' (crypto_rigor.py). That argument only covers BIJECTIVE 29→29 substitution. A many-to-few encoding — 29 rune symbols serving as homophones/variable-length code for a smaller plaintext alphabet (reduced English, a checkerboard, hex/base-k data) — flattens IoC BY DESIGN, and the encoder's free choice among homophones is a natural way to implement the ~83% soft no-repeat rule by hand (pick a different homophone when a repeat looms). It is the one classical, actually-SOLVABLE cipher class that reproduces both statistics and was never tested. My repeated-word probe (481 excess repeats vs 482.9±8.9 random) kills fixed word-codebooks but NOT letter-level homophony with free choice, which predicts exactly chance-level word repeats.
proposals.fits_constraints: Uniform choice among homophones → IoC·N = 1.000 flat; repeat-avoiding homophone selection → doublet deficit, soft (author slips ~1 in 6) — matches 0.664% without any keystream at all.
proposals.how_to_test: Simulated-annealing / EM over surjective maps 29→k (k = 5..26) maximizing class-stream quadgram score under multiple language models (English, Latin, Old English) plus structured-data models; validate the searcher first on synthetic homophonic ciphertext (rig tradition); score real pages against a 1000-shuffle control envelope. Also test the natural partitions first (futhorc aetts, vowel/consonant, index mod k, prime residues).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: An optimized partition whose class-stream score on real ciphertext exceeds the shuffled-control 99.9th percentile and generalizes across held-out pages. Failure across all k and language models = class closed with a measured null.
proposals.already_covered_check: crypto_rigor.py's homophonic elimination is a one-line IoC-preservation argument valid only for bijections; no many-to-few map, straddling-checkerboard, or partition search appears anywhere in the ledger or analysis/.

proposals.name: Targeted doublet-site image count-audit (haplography test — the cheap falsifier of the entire 'engineered filter' edifice)
proposals.rationale: The doublet deficit is the load-bearing anomaly: Campaign X's autokey exclusion, Campaign XI's 83% filter, and the OTP verdict all sit on it. Transcription is 'verified 3 ways' — but at GLYPH-IDENTITY level, by lineages sharing one origin (the project's own FINDINGS-FOR-SOLVERS admits an independent re-read is the one unverified input). Rune-COUNT errors — a transcriber merging doubled runes (haplography, the most common scribal/transcription error class) — would be invisible to identity-level checks and would manufacture exactly this deficit. The strong counterevidence is the solved-page control (same lineage, normal doublet rates), and solved pages got decryption feedback that corrects counts — unsolved pages NEVER had that error-correcting loop. Full vision re-transcription failed on dense pages, but this needs only ~300 tiny crops.
proposals.fits_constraints: Challenges whether the 0.66% statistic is real rather than needing to reproduce it; flat IoC is untouched either way (IoC is insensitive to ~2.7% merged runes).
proposals.how_to_test: From the SHA1-verified 400-DPI renders, crop the neighborhoods of all 86 canon doublet sites plus ~200 random adjacent-pair sites; audit RUNE COUNT (not identity) per crop by eye and/or the existing template matcher (which was 69.5% on full pages but is near-trivial on 3-5 rune crops). Also cross-check per-page rune totals against line-count × runes-per-line from the images.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any systematic finding of adjacent-equal rune pairs in the images where canon records a single rune. Even ~20 confirmed merges moves the true doublet rate toward random and forces re-running Campaign X — ciphertext autokey (IoC 1.000, doublets 3.45%) would come straight back onto the table. Clean audit = the deficit is finally count-verified and the OTP verdict hardens.
proposals.already_covered_check: Campaign V verified glyph identity and lineage agreement; the vision avenue was closed for FULL-page re-transcription only. No count-level audit targeted at doublet-relevant sites exists anywhere in analysis/transcription/ or the ledger.

proposals.name: Generator-fingerprint suite: pin down HOW the filtered stream was produced (finish what I started above)
proposals.rationale: Six probes I ran during this review already sharpened the mechanism beyond Campaign XI: the filter is stream-level (uniform across word/line/page boundaries — 0/54 page-boundary doublets suggests one continuous generation pass in book order), exact-match-only (no delta±1 or visual-similarity avoidance), memoryless (no lag-2 excess), unbiased (doublet composition chi2 26.0/df 28 = clean rejection sampling), driftless. This is a MACHINE signature, not hand discipline — which itself is new information (Campaign XI left 'by-hand discipline' open). Remaining probes can distinguish rejection-resampling from shuffle-bag/anti-repeat PRNG designs, and every constraint shrinks the space of generator code an author could have used.
proposals.fits_constraints: These are measurements OF the two constraint statistics' fine structure; by construction consistent.
proposals.how_to_test: (1) Conditional next-rune distribution following each rune value (a resampler leaves uniform-over-28; a Fisher-Yates-with-swap or shuffle-bag leaves detectable under-dispersion); (2) windowed chi2 under-dispersion sweep (shuffle-bag = sub-random symbol balance in windows); (3) recompute everything over the ~75-page community corpus (the project's own flagged gap) — 40% more data doubles power on all fine-structure tests, including the underpowered page-boundary result; (4) doublet rate conditioned on preceding doublet distance (geometric-gap test: soft-iid filter predicts geometric; I saw no periodicity but never fit the gap law).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any deviation from the iid-rejection-sampler predictions (non-uniform post-repeat distribution, windowed under-dispersion, non-geometric gaps) = a NEW exploitable regularity in the keystream and a fingerprint of specific generator code; full conformance = the strongest possible confirmation of the OTP verdict, with the 'hand-generated pad' loophole (and its human-bias attack surface) formally closed.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger's fine structure stops at the lag-1 hole and the aggregate 83% suppression number; boundary conditioning, lag-k, composition, near-miss, gap-law, dispersion, and cross-page continuity appear nowhere — the first six I ran myself today and they produced new (null and non-null) results.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The ciphertext is exactly the separator-stripped rune sequence — word boundaries, word lengths, and layout carry no information (the rig's runes_to_indices() throws '•' away before every attack and every statistic).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Every solved LP page enciphers word-by-word, so ciphertext boundaries = plaintext boundaries there; I measured the unsolved pages' 3,316-word length distribution and it statistically matches solved English pages (chi2 6.3, df~11). Either the plaintext word skeleton is publicly exposed, or the author faked an English-like skeleton — both are facts the project has never engaged.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: A key-independent attack surface (skeleton fingerprinting, positional cribs, function-word rhythm) exists entirely outside the 'OTP = unsolvable' argument, which only covers the rune stream. This is the largest genuine reopening.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: Homophonic ciphers are eliminated because 'substitution preserves IoC'.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: That argument is only valid for bijective 29→29 maps. Many-to-few homophonic or variable-rate encodings (29 runes over a smaller plaintext alphabet) flatten IoC by design, and homophone choice-freedom naturally implements the observed soft no-repeat rule.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: A classically SOLVABLE cipher class reproduces both signature statistics and was never tested — the 'only a full-length keystream fits' inference has an untested competitor.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The 0.66% doublet rate is a true property of the authored ciphertext (count-level transcription correctness).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Transcription was verified at glyph-identity level by shared-origin lineages; haplography (merging doubled runes) is invisible to identity checks. Solved-page controls mitigate strongly (same lineage, normal rates) but solved pages had decryption feedback correcting counts; unsolved pages never did.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: The whole mechanism edifice collapses: Campaign X's autokey exclusion rests on the deficit, so ciphertext autokey (IoC 1.000, doublets ~3.45%) — the community's decade hypothesis — comes straight back. Lowest probability, highest consequence; closable for ~300 image crops.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The 'untried public keytext' avenue is the one productive open path (Still-genuinely-open #1).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: The project's own Campaign X table shows ANY English-letter-frequency running key forces IoC·N ≈ 1.06 (vs observed 1.000, wildly significant at n=12,956) and ~3.3-4.1% doublets — so plain additive keytexts were mechanistically dead before Campaigns XII/XIII swept 97 of them. The frontier framing contradicts the project's own math.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Two-sided: (a) resources keep going to foreordained nulls; (b) conversely, the '112 texts eliminated' claim covers ONLY additive/Atbash combining — if a keytext was used through any non-additive combiner, none of those eliminations apply, so the eliminated set is narrower than advertised.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The plaintext is English at rune granularity (all scoring is English quadgrams over Gematria transliteration).
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Cicada precedent is English, but the scorer is load-bearing for every sweep-null; Latin, Old English, or structured non-text payloads would sail under the -5.2 threshold. IoC flatness conclusions are language-independent, but all 'nothing readable' verdicts are not.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Sweep nulls (and any future homophonic/skeleton search) under-detect; cheap to hedge by adding 2-3 alternate language models to the scorer.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The no-repeat filter is cryptographic in purpose (a pad-generation discipline), i.e., there IS a message under the unsolved pages.
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Exact-repeat avoidance is the classic signature of procedural 'looks-random-to-humans' generation (shuffle-style anti-repeat), and my probes show a machine-like, aesthetic-grade filter (exact-match-only, boundary-blind, unbiased). A designed decoy body with English-like word skeleton fits everything and would make LP2 an art object / time-lock rather than a cipher.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: No amount of cryptanalysis can succeed; the honest verdict would shift from 'unsolvable without the key' to 'possibly nothing to solve' — worth stating as a live branch rather than a footnote, since it changes what 'progress' means.

overclaimed_as_closed: Substitution/homophonic (ledger row C): only bijective substitution was eliminated; many-to-few homophonic and variable-rate encodings — which flatten IoC by design and can implement the soft no-repeat rule via homophone choice — were never tested.

Transcription 'verified correct 3 ways': verified at glyph-IDENTITY level by lineages sharing one origin; rune-COUNT correctness (haplography) at the 86 doublet-relevant sites was never audited, and unsolved pages lack the decryption feedback that validated solved-page counts.

Metadata channels implicitly closed by the 'every attack must reproduce IoC+doublet' gate: word boundaries/lengths, layout, and doublet positions were never enumerated as channels. (I tested two today — doublet-gap encoding: null; codebook word-repeats: exactly chance — but the word-length skeleton channel is open and English-like.)

The keytext eliminations (112 texts) as meaningful narrowing: by the project's own Campaign X numbers (English running key → IoC·N 1.061, doublets 4.1%) those texts were excluded a priori, and the eliminations cover additive/Atbash combining only — the claim 'tried' overstates coverage of each text and understates the mechanism argument that made trying them moot.
'Autokey excluded' stated unconditionally: the exclusion is conditional on the doublet deficit being a genuine ciphertext property (see count-audit); cheap to make unconditional, currently isn't.
highest_ev_experiment: The word-length skeleton attack (proposal 1). It is the only fresh avenue with a plausible direct path to plaintext: the unsolved pages' '•'-delimited word-length distribution matches solved English pages (chi2 6.3 — measured today, never before computed), solved-LP precedent says boundaries are plaintext-true, and a ~20-word length window is a ~40-50-bit fingerprint searchable against the 112 already-downloaded corpus texts (reused as plaintext candidates, not keys) plus Gutenberg at scale — key-independent, a weekend of compute, with a calibrated false-positive control. Runner-up on pure EV-per-dollar: the 86-site doublet count-audit (proposal 3), because it is the cheapest experiment that could collapse the entire 'engineered filter → OTP → unsolvable' chain if it finds even a handful of merged doublets — and hardens it permanently if clean.
verdict_on_unsolvable: Agree for the modeled class, but the universal wording is overstated. Within the class the project actually analyzed — a cipher over the separator-stripped rune stream at rune granularity, with plaintext-independent or output-filtered full-length keying — the verdict is sound and my own probes strengthened it (the filter looks machine-applied, exact-match-only, unbiased, continuous across the whole book; every fine-structure channel I tested for exploitable memory came back clean). The overstatement is in scope, not math: (1) 'information-theoretically unsolvable' silently conditions on the doublet deficit being real at the transcription-count level, which was never audited where it matters and is the pillar under the autokey exclusion; (2) two in-principle-solvable constructions — many-to-few homophonic encoding and the word-length metadata channel — reproduce both signature statistics and are absent from the elimination ledger, so 'for any chosen plaintext a valid structureless key exists' is true but not exhaustive of how meaning could be recoverable; (3) the stated open frontier is internally inconsistent — English keytexts are doubly excluded by the project's own IoC and doublet numbers, so 'more keytexts' is the one listed-open avenue that is actually closed, while the genuinely open items (skeleton channel, homophonic-downward, count-audit, 75-page corpus) are unlisted. Net: the wall is probably real, but the honest claim is 'unsolvable within every class we modeled' — and at least three cheap experiments stand between that and the unconditional sentence currently in the ledger.

lens: cryptanalysis — constructions that produce flat IoC 1.000 + doublet deficit without being a pure external OTP; plus soundness audit of the eliminations. NOTE: I did not only propose — I ran the cheap gateway tests against the repo's own data (scripts in scratchpad/redteam_probe.py) and report the measured outcomes inline.

proposals.name: Generalized-combiner ciphertext-feedback family (permutation-chain c_i = p_i + g(c_{i-1}), full Latin-square tableau chain c_i = T[p_i][c_{i-1}], and doublet-avoidant homophonic-class re-encoding)
proposals.rationale: This was the strongest 'fits everything, still breakable' candidate: a doubly-stochastic feedback chain gives EXACTLY uniform stationary output (IoC.N = 1.000, max entropy), a tableau whose fixed-points map to rare plaintext letters gives a ~0.66% doublet rate (the project's own DOUBLET-INVESTIGATION section 3 noted rare-rune frequencies EO=0.67% match the observed rate), and the marginal difference distribution stays flat-with-hole if g is a complete mapping. Campaign X only ever simulated ADDITIVE autokey combiners, so 'autokey excluded' did not cover this. Decryption would be a 29-permutation or 841-cell hill-climb (Zodiac-homophonic-class difficulty). The discriminating statistic no doc ever reports is the full 29x29 ciphertext BIGRAM matrix: every member of this family forces the conditional distribution P(c_i | c_{i-1}) to be a permuted plaintext distribution (conditional IoC.N ~1.6-1.8), while a filtered OTP forces it flat.
proposals.fits_constraints: Doubly stochastic transition kernel => unigram IoC.N exactly 1.000; rare-letter fixed points => doublet ~0.66% (soft, plaintext-dependent); complete-mapping g => marginal differences flat with hole at 0; chain never resets => continuity across page joins. Fits every statistic the project published.
proposals.how_to_test: Compute the 29x29 bigram matrix over the 12,955 adjacent pairs; measure conditional IoC.N per row and off-diagonal chi-square vs uniform. If structured, anneal g (29! space, trivial) / T (Latin-square-constrained) against quadgram score, validated first on synthetic ct.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: EXECUTED during this review: bigram matrix is dead flat — off-diagonal chi2 = 841 vs df 811 (z = +0.75 sigma), conditional IoC.N mean 1.026 vs 1.037 for the uniform-with-hole null, bigram entropy 9.642/9.716 bits. The entire first-order feedback-combiner family (and homophonic-class encoding over any structured inner stream) is hereby FALSIFIED with new data — this should be added to the elimination ledger as a genuinely new closure, because until this measurement the family was live and the 'autokey excluded' claim did not cover it.
proposals.already_covered_check: Not in the ledger: Campaign X simulated only additive (+K) autokey variants; no document reports any second-order/bigram/conditional statistic — only the diagonal (doublets) and the marginal difference distribution, neither of which constrains this family.

proposals.name: Seeded-generator keystream with encryption-time skip filter (hash-chain / HMAC-DRBG / RC4 / AES-CTR / MD5-SHA1-SHA256 chains from a Cicada-derived seed dictionary), decrypted with filter-aware beam
proposals.rationale: The ledger's 'PRNG keystreams dead' row covers only BBS/LCG/Mersenne-Twister. The 2014-era-obvious way to make a 12,956-symbol pad is iterated hashing or a stream cipher keyed by a phrase — never tried. Critically, NO keystream elimination in the ledger used filter-aware decryption: if the author generated a stream and applied the observed skip-on-repeat rule at encryption time, key consumption desyncs from position, and every rigid-alignment trial ever run would miss even a correct generator. The skip events are recoverable by the decryptor: a skip occurred at i iff c_{i-1} - c_i = k_j - k_{j+1}, so decryption is a low-branching beam (the ~17% soft slips add ~0.66% branch noise, exactly the observed doublets). My doublet forensics today (gap KS vs geometric D=0.065, no first/second-half drift 40 vs 46, identities spread over 28 runes) show the slip process is memoryless and stationary — consistent with a machine-made pad used with a hand-applied skip discipline.
proposals.fits_constraints: Hash/stream-cipher output is uniform => IoC.N 1.000 and flat bigram (both now measured true); skip-on-repeat applied at encryption => doublet deficit, strictly lag-1 (measured today: lag-2..12 all ~3.4%, only lag-1 suppressed — exactly this signature); hand slips ~1-in-6 => soft 83%.
proposals.how_to_test: One harness: seeds = {37-string Cicada dict, 3301 variants, magic-square constants, the AN END 512-bit hash string, solved-page phrases} x generators {MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256/SHA-512 chain & counter, HMAC-DRBG, RC4, AES-CTR} x mod-29 reductions {byte mod 29, rejection >=232, 16-bit word} x {filter-aware skip beam on/off, sign, Atbash}. ~10-20k configs x 55 pages, hours. Validate the beam on synthetic filtered ct first (rig-style).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any page scoring above the validated -5.2 quadgram threshold (or IoC.N of decrypt > 1.3, language-agnostic) = break; clean sweep = the family joins the ledger with named seeds/generators. The harness self-validates by recovering a planted synthetic key.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger row A lists only 'BBS / LCG / Mersenne Twister seeded' — no hash-based generators, no RC4/AES, and no keystream trial anywhere used skip-aware (filter-consistent) decryption; all assumed rigid position alignment.

proposals.name: pp49-51 payload as PRF seed / stream-cipher key for the runic pages (payload EXPANDED, not used directly)
proposals.rationale: The 2048-bit high-entropy blob was tested as a DIRECT polyalphabetic key (337,944 configs), as text, format, preimage, XOR key, image — but never in its most cryptographically natural role: a 256-byte SEED/KEY expanded through a PRF to the ~13k-rune pad. 256 bytes is exactly an RC4 max key / an RSA-2048-size blob / a DRBG seed. It is the one internal object whose meaning the project itself concludes 'is only recoverable with external context' — unless the context is the surrounding pages and the payload is the pad's seed, making LP2 self-contained after all (matching how every solved page was self-contained, a Cicada design invariant the project itself documented).
proposals.fits_constraints: PRF expansion of a high-entropy seed => uniform keystream => IoC.N 1.000 + flat bigram (both measured); the doublet filter sits at encryption exactly as in the previous proposal; strictly-lag-1 suppression consistent.
proposals.how_to_test: Expand canon_256.bin via {RC4(key=payload), AES-128/256-CTR (key||IV from payload), SHA-256/SHA-1/MD5 in counter and chain mode, HMAC-DRBG(seed=payload)} x 3 mod-29 reductions x {skip-beam on/off, sign, Atbash, per-page vs continuous stream, payload forward/reversed}; decrypt all 55 pages, score quadgram + language-agnostic IoC. A few hundred configs — an afternoon.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any decrypt over -5.2 (or decrypt IoC.N > 1.3) = solve; all-null = a new named ledger row ('payload as PRF seed: dead'), closing the payload's last natural cryptographic role.
proposals.already_covered_check: Campaign VII tested the payload as a direct polyalphabetic key over the runes and Campaign XI as alt-base direct key; no campaign ever expanded it through a PRF/stream cipher. Distinct construction, absent from the ledger.

proposals.name: Word-length / separator metadata battery (the plaintext-visible side channel the cipher never touched)
proposals.rationale: LP2 retains word (-), clause (.), line (/) separators — 2,589 word boundaries of plaintext METADATA that no additive cipher hides. The project used separators only for boundary-doublet tests. Word-length distribution, word-length bigrams, and sentence-length stats directly test (a) whether a natural-language plaintext exists at all (vs 'filtered random filler / designed unsolvable', which the project lists as possible but never tested), and (b) WHICH language (English-in-futhorc has multi-letter runes TH/EO/NG/AE/IA/EA, giving a distinctive compressed word-length profile measurable from the solved pages).
proposals.fits_constraints: Orthogonal to the rune stream — makes no claim about IoC or doublets, so trivially consistent; it tests the load-bearing assumption UNDER the OTP verdict (that there is an English message).
proposals.how_to_test: Chi-square word-length and word-length-bigram distributions of unsolved pages vs (i) solved-LP plaintext, (ii) English/Latin/Old-English corpora mapped through Gematria Primus, (iii) random-filler models (uniform lengths, copied-length templates). Also compare per-page word-count/line-length structure against solved pages.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Match to solved-LP English profile => plaintext is real English prose (constrains every future attack, enables word-boundary pruning in the beam decoders above); match to nothing => first positive evidence for the filler/pad hypothesis, overturning the implicit 'there is a message' assumption. Either outcome is information; the test cannot 'fail silently'.
proposals.already_covered_check: STRUCTURE-FINDINGS used separators solely to show doublet suppression crosses boundaries; no word-length or language-fingerprint analysis appears in any campaign doc or the ledger.

proposals.name: Skip-tolerant re-verification of the 112 'eliminated' keytexts (soundness patch, not a lead)
proposals.rationale: Every running-key elimination decrypted with rigid alignment and straight addition. If the doublet filter operated by SKIPPING a key letter when output would repeat (the natural hand rule), alignment drifts at ~3.4% of positions and cascades — a correct keytext would have scored in the noise band in every sweep run to date. Honest caveat cutting the other way: the project's own Campaign X shows English-key-over-English-plaintext yields IoC.N ~1.061, not the observed 1.000 (and my bigram-flat measurement adds a second independent strike), so plain English keytexts are disfavored REGARDLESS of alignment — which also means the ledger's 'open avenue #1: untried keytext' is nearly self-contradicted by the project's own statistics and deserves an explicit note.
proposals.fits_constraints: Skip-filter at encryption produces the doublet deficit and lag-1-only suppression; the IoC 1.000 constraint is only satisfied if the keytext is near-uniform in rune space (numeric/tabular text) or the plaintext is not frequency-typical — stated honestly as the surviving corner.
proposals.how_to_test: Beam decoder with key-skip transitions (penalized), validated on synthetic skip-filtered running-key ct, re-run over the existing ~117-text corpus (fetch scripts already in analysis/campaign12).
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Beam finds an over-threshold decrypt some text where rigid alignment scored ~-6 => break; all-null => the keytext eliminations become sound against filtered use, and the ledger caveat is discharged.
proposals.already_covered_check: attack.py runningkey is strictly rigid-alignment additive (verified by reading the code); find_interrupters exists only in the vigenere path and only refines candidates that already scored > -6.2 on a no-interrupter pass — a filtered hit would never reach the refine gate.

proposals.name: LP2-as-pad inversion: the unsolved pages ARE key material, not a message
proposals.rationale: A pure inversion never in the ledger: Cicada distributed the PAD in plain sight (a 'book of law' you must possess), and the message it decrypts is/was elsewhere (e.g., the AN END page). The no-repeat filter then has a tradecraft rationale — adjacent-repeat-free pads are easier to hand-copy and index without line-skipping errors. Testable against the finite set of other Cicada artifacts.
proposals.fits_constraints: If LP2 IS the random keystream, IoC.N 1.000, max entropy, flat bigram, and a generation-time no-repeat discipline (soft 83%) are all properties of the artifact itself — the constraints are satisfied by construction rather than explained by a cipher.
proposals.how_to_test: Use the 12,956-rune stream (fwd/rev, +/-, Atbash) as running key against every other machine-readable Cicada object: unsolved 2012/2013 fragments, the 512-bit AN END hash bytes, the pp49-51 payload (payload minus LP2-stream => structure?), onion names, PGP message bodies. Finite, hours.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any counterpart object decrypting to over-threshold English/structure = paradigm-level break; all-null = documented closure of the inversion (it is finitely testable precisely because the candidate message set is small).
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger covers page-on-page keying WITHIN LP2 and payload-as-key OVER LP2; nowhere is the full LP2 stream used as key material against non-LP2 artifacts.

proposals.name: Extended-corpus second-order rerun (ingest the ~75-page community corpus; kappa/in-depth/bigram across old-new joins)
proposals.rationale: The project flagged ingesting the ~4-20 newly transcribed pages as bookkeeping; the red-team point is sharper: key REUSE (the only internal weakness a pad system can have) may exist only between pages 0-55 and pages never ingested — no coincidence test has ever seen those pairs. My full kappa spectrum today (lags 1-6000 on raw, F-removed, difference, and diff-of-F-removed indexings: no hit above 4.5 sigma that survives confirmation — two blips at lags 3915/5091 in the F-removed stream reached 4.6-5.0 sigma but their aligned-difference IoC.N is 1.002-1.005, i.e., NOT English-difference, so noise) closes reuse within 0-55; the extended corpus is the only place a reuse signal can still hide.
proposals.fits_constraints: Makes no mechanism claim; targets the one weakness (reuse) that flat IoC and the doublet deficit cannot exclude across data the rig has never loaded.
proposals.how_to_test: Verify + ingest the additional community-transcribed pages (3-way check as before); rerun the kappa spectrum to full corpus length, page-on-page in-depth, bigram matrix, and re-test the lag-3915/5091 blips on the enlarged stream.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any lag with kappa > 5 sigma AND aligned-difference IoC.N > ~1.02 (English-difference signature) = in-depth attackable key reuse; otherwise the OTP verdict extends to the full book and the blips die.
proposals.already_covered_check: Ledger lists ingestion as a coverage gap but prescribes no cross-corpus coincidence attack; the full kappa spectrum itself appears in no project doc (I ran the 0-55 version today).

proposals.name: Language-agnostic and non-English re-scoring of all sweep bests (Old English / Latin / IoC-of-decrypt)
proposals.rationale: Every elimination is conditional on an English quadgram scorer. A runic book plausibly carries Old English or Latin; a correct key with non-English plaintext would have scored ~-6 ('noise') in every sweep and been recorded as a null. Cheap insurance: rescore, don't re-search.
proposals.fits_constraints: Scoring-layer change only; no mechanism claim, so trivially consistent with both statistics.
proposals.how_to_test: Build OE and Latin quadgram tables mapped through Gematria Primus; re-score the archived best-candidate decrypts of every prior sweep (vigauto, runningkey, keystream, autokey brute) plus add decrypt-IoC.N > 1.3 as a language-blind gate to all future harnesses.
proposals.falsifiable_signal: Any archived 'null' whose decrypt scores high under OE/Latin or shows IoC.N > 1.3 = missed hit; none = the English-plaintext assumption is discharged for everything already run.
proposals.already_covered_check: All scoring in attack.py and campaign scripts uses the single English quadgram model (verified in code); no multi-language or language-agnostic rescoring pass exists in the ledger.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: 'Autokey is excluded' (Campaign X) — generalized to all self-keying schemes
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Only ADDITIVE combiners were simulated; ciphertext-feedback through a keyed permutation or Latin-square tableau is doubly stochastic (IoC.N exactly 1.000) and can pin doublets at ~0.66% via rare-letter fixed points — it fit every statistic the project had published.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: A breakable 29-permutation/841-cell hand cipher would have been sitting behind an 'excluded' ledger row. (I closed this today with the previously-unmeasured bigram matrix: off-diagonal chi2 z=+0.75, conditional IoC.N 1.026 — the family is now genuinely dead, but it was an overclaim until measured.)

flawed_assumptions.assumption: The doublet filter was applied to a pre-generated keystream without perturbing key consumption (rigid alignment in every decryption trial)
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: The equally natural mechanism is skip-on-repeat at encryption time, which desyncs key index from position at ~3.4% of positions and cascades; no sweep in the repo (keytexts, keystreams, payload-as-key) used skip-aware decoding.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Every keystream and running-key elimination is unsound against filtered use — a correct generator or text would have scored in the noise band. Fix = the skip-aware beam in proposals 2/3/5.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: Plaintext is English prose, catchable by the English quadgram scorer
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: Solved pages were English, but a futhorc book plausibly carries Old English or Latin, and pages could carry structured data (like pp49-51) rather than prose; both score as 'noise' under the single scorer used everywhere.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: All ~112 keytext nulls and every keystream null are conditional; a correct key may already sit in the archived 'best candidates' unrecognized. Cheap fix: multi-language + IoC-of-decrypt rescoring (proposal 8).

flawed_assumptions.assumption: 'Soft ~83% suppression' is a mechanism parameter (a filter that tolerates repeats ~17% of the time)
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: 83% is a fit parameter of one generative model.
