Hard-rule alternatives existed: F-as-escape-null (insert variant now KILLED by my straddle test — straddle pairs are 2.86% not ~100%; substitute-and-rekey variant weakened but not dead), and hard-rule-plus-error models. My forensics (geometric gaps D=0.065, no half-to-half drift, uniform slip identities) support memoryless softness but cannot distinguish hand-slips from probabilistic software.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: Wrong generation model misprices attacks: a hard algorithmic rule points to software generation and raises the prior on recoverable-seed attacks (proposals 2/3); a hand discipline points external. The 0.66% also cannot be transcription noise (0.8% glyph error rate creates only ~0.06% spurious doublets).

flawed_assumptions.assumption: There is a plaintext at all (LP2 is a message, not key material or filler)
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: A filtered-uniform stream with real word/line/page formatting is exactly what deliberate filler OR a distributed one-time pad would look like; nothing internal measured so far distinguishes these from an enciphered message.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: The 'only realistic path is the external key' conclusion would be aimed at a lock with nothing behind it, or at the wrong half of the system (proposal 6 inverts it). The word-length metadata battery (proposal 4) is the one internal test that bears on this and has never been run.

flawed_assumptions.assumption: Pages 0-55 are the object; statistics over them characterize the system
flawed_assumptions.why_might_be_wrong: The community corpus is now ~75 pages; coincidence/key-reuse tests have never seen pairs involving the un-ingested pages, and reuse is the only internal weakness a pad system can have.
flawed_assumptions.consequence_if_wrong: An in-depth (two pages under one keystream segment) could exist entirely outside the analyzed window; all 'no reuse' claims are scoped narrower than stated. Cheap fix: proposal 7.

overclaimed_as_closed: 'Autokey excluded' — only additive (+K) combiners were simulated; generalized-combiner feedback (permutation g / Latin-square tableau) fit every published statistic and was live until this review's bigram-matrix measurement (now measured flat: off-diag chi2 z=+0.75 sigma, conditional IoC.N 1.026 vs 1.037 null — family genuinely closed TODAY, not by Campaign X).

'Running keys from 112+ texts eliminated' — sound only under rigid alignment, straight-additive combining, and English-quadgram scoring; filtered/skip usage of those same texts and non-English plaintext were never covered (mitigated but not removed by the IoC-1.061-vs-1.000 argument against English keys).

'PRNG keystreams dead' — covers only BBS/LCG/Mersenne-Twister; hash-chain/HMAC/RC4/AES-CTR generators (the era-typical choices) were never tried, and no keystream trial anywhere used filter-aware decryption.

'Substitution / homophonic dead because substitution preserves IoC' — the argument does not cover doublet-avoidant many-to-one re-encoding layers, which flatten IoC AND suppress doublets by design; that family also survived until today's bigram null (and its only surviving corner — encoding over an already-flat inner stream — is unbreakable anyway, which should be stated as the actual reason it's closed).

'No period, no keystream structure' (DOUBLET-INVESTIGATION) — was inferred from unigram IoC and marginal difference stats; an any-alphabet full kappa spectrum appears nowhere in the ledger. I ran it today (lags 1-6000 on four indexings: raw, F-removed, difference, diff-of-F-removed): null except two sub-confirmatory blips (F-removed lags 3915/5091, 4.6-5.0 sigma nominal, aligned-difference IoC.N 1.002-1.005 = not English-difference => noise), so the conclusion stands but the ledger row overstated its evidentiary basis.

'The F interrupter behaves as a null, not a carrier' — the doublet-escape role of F was never tested; my straddle measurement (pairs across a deleted F: 2.86% doublets; direct pairs: 0.68%) kills the insert-null variant decisively but leaves the substitute-and-rekey variant only weakened, and F is measurably a full participant in the no-repeat rule (suppression strictly lag-1 including F-adjacency).
highest_ev_experiment: One combined harness: the seeded-generator battery (proposals 2+3) — expand {37-string Cicada dictionary, 3301 variants, magic-square constants, the AN END 512-bit hash string, and above all the pp49-51 256-byte payload} through {RC4, AES-CTR, MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256/SHA-512 in chain and counter modes, HMAC-DRBG} x {3 mod-29 reductions} x {skip-aware beam decryption on/off, sign, Atbash, continuous vs per-page}, scoring all 55 pages with the validated quadgram model PLUS a language-agnostic decrypt-IoC gate; validate the skip-beam on synthetic filtered ciphertext first. Rationale: after this review's own measurements (flat bigram matrix, null kappa spectra, strictly-lag-1 memoryless-stationary filter), the ONLY surviving internal-solve family is 'machine-generated pad from a recoverable seed with the filter applied at encryption' — a finite, never-tested, afternoon-sized family whose hit value is a complete solve; and the payload is the single most plausible seed object in the entire corpus. (Cheapest pure-information runner-up: the word-length metadata battery, proposal 4, which settles whether a natural-language plaintext exists at all.)
verdict_on_unsolvable: Agree with the OTP-class characterization, and this review STRENGTHENS it with new measurements: the bigram matrix is flat (killing the last statistically-consistent breakable cipher family — feedback-through-permutation/tableau and homophonic-class encodings), the any-alphabet kappa spectrum is null to lag 6000 on four indexings (no key reuse/periodicity of any kind within pages 0-55), and the filter is strictly lag-1, memoryless and stationary. However, 'information-theoretically unsolvable without an external key' is mildly OVERSTATED as an unconditional claim: it is conditional on (a) the keystream not being algorithmically generated from a recoverable seed — a finite family (hash-chains, RC4/AES-CTR, payload-as-seed, all with filter-aware decoding) that the ledger has never tested, (b) the plaintext existing and being prose (never tested; word-length metadata can test it), and (c) the eliminations being sound against filter-induced key-schedule desync (they are not, as currently implemented). The defensible phrasing: 'indistinguishable from a filtered one-time pad by every first- and second-order statistic now measured; internally solvable only if the pad was machine-generated from a guessable seed — a small, enumerable, not-yet-exhausted family — otherwise external-key-only.' Also flag: open avenue #1 (untried public keytext) as currently framed is nearly self-contradicted by the project's own numbers (English key over English plaintext predicts IoC.N ~1.061 and ~3.3% doublets; observed 1.000 and 0.66%), so it should be demoted or reframed around non-frequency-typical key material rather than more literature.
